factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, particularly the conflict between Russia and Ukraine, are creating potential risks but have not had a material impact yet.

evidence.text: The ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine and the related sanctions and export controls have adversely affected economic conditions in Eastern Europe and certain global industry sectors dependent on those countries. We have limited physical operations and sales in Russia and Ukraine and, to date, have not experienced a material adverse impact on our results of operations or financial condition. Further escalation or prolonged conflict may amplify several of the risks identified in Part I, Item 1A. 'Risk Factors' in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: General Economic Conditions

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The conflict between Russia and Ukraine poses potential risks but has not yet caused material harm.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are increasing interest expenses, which is a headwind.

evidence.text: Interest expense was $38.9 million for the three month period ended March 31, 2023, an increase of $19.9 million, compared to $19.0 million in the same three month period in 2022. The increase was primarily due to an increase in the weighted-average interest rate, partially offset by the prepayment of the Euro Term Loan on June 30, 2022 and the interest rate derivative contracts discussed in Note 13 'Hedging Activities and Derivative Instruments' to our unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Form 10-Q. The weighted average interest rate, including the impact of the interest rate derivative contracts, was approximately 5.2% for the three month period ended March 31, 2023 and 1.9% in the same three month period in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Results of Continuing Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising interest rates have led to a significant increase in interest expenses, acting as a headwind.

factors.summary: The company is restructuring its workforce, leading to changes in headcount.

evidence.text: We continue to execute business transformation initiatives. A key element of those initiatives are restructuring programs within our Industrial Technologies and Services and Precision and Science Technologies segments, as well as at the Corporate level.
evidence.subsection: Restructuring and Other Business Transformation Initiatives

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring workforce indicates change but no clear positive or negative impact yet.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue.

evidence.text: Segment Orders for the three month period ended March 31, 2023 were $1,450.3 million, an increase of $157.5 million, or 12.2%, compared to $1,292.8 million in the same three month period in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing order backlog suggests strong demand and potential future revenue growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, as evidenced by the increase in Segment Adjusted EBITDA Margins.

evidence.text: Segment Adjusted EBITDA Margin increased 240 basis points to 26.2% from 23.8% in 2022. The increase in Segment Adjusted EBITDA was primarily due to higher pricing of $106.9 million or 43.2%, higher organic sales volume of $57.1 million or 23.1%, and acquisitions of $18.6 million or 7.5%, partially offset by unfavorable cost inflation and product mix of $46.1 million or 18.6%, higher selling and administrative costs of $21.3 million or 8.6% and unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $12.7 million or 5.1%.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments improves profitability and sustainability.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by higher organic sales volumes and increased demand for certain products.

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Revenues was due to higher organic volumes of $147.3 million or 14.2%, and acquisitions of $69.9 million or 6.7%, partially offset by unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $46.5 million or 4.5%.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing unit volumes driven by higher organic sales and demand indicate growth.

factors.summary: The Industrial Technologies and Services segment is growing fastest, while the Precision and Science Technologies segment is experiencing a slight decline in orders but stable revenue growth.

evidence.text: Segment Orders for the three month period ended March 31, 2023 were $326.5 million, a decrease of $10.6 million, or 3.1%, compared to $337.1 million in the same three month period in 2022. The decrease in Segment Orders was due to the unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $11.0 million or 3.3% and organic decline of $5.8 million or 1.7%, partially offset by acquisitions of $6.2 million or 1.8%. Segment Revenues for the three month period ended March 31, 2023 were $312.1 million, an increase of $14.7 million, or 4.9%, compared to $297.4 million in the same three month period in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with some growth and some decline, leading to an overall neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, as evidenced by higher organic sales volumes and efficiency gains.

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Adjusted EBITDA was primarily due to higher organic sales volume of $57.1 million or 23.1%, and acquisitions of $18.6 million or 7.5%, partially offset by unfavorable cost inflation and product mix of $46.1 million or 18.6%, higher selling and administrative costs of $21.3 million or 8.6% and unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $12.7 million or 5.1%.

sentiment.label: positive

evidence.text: The unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $46.5 million or 4.5%. The increase in Segment Adjusted EBITDA was primarily due to higher pricing of $106.9 million or 43.2%, higher organic sales volume of $57.1 million or 23.1%, and acquisitions of $18.6 million or 7.5%, partially offset by unfavorable cost inflation and product mix of $46.1 million or 18.6%, higher selling and administrative costs of $21.3 million or 8.6% and unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $12.7 million or 5.1%.

sentiment.rationale: Evidence highlights unfavorable foreign currency impacts, cost inflation, and working capital challenges, indicating near-term headwinds.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on capital expenditures, strategic plans, and possible acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our principal uses of cash will be to provide working capital, meet debt service requirements, fund capital expenditures, dividend payments, and finance strategic plans, including possible acquisitions. We may also seek to finance capital expenditures under capital leases or other debt arrangements that provide liquidity or favorable borrowing terms.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: The supply chain is improving, with increases in inventories and efficient management of working capital.

evidence.text: The increase in operating working capital is primarily due to higher accounts receivable, higher inventories and lower accounts payable, partially offset by higher contract liabilities and lower contract assets.
evidence.subsection: Working Capital

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving supply chain health with higher inventories and efficient working capital management suggests a positive trend.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, supported by higher organic sales volumes and strategic investments.

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Adjusted EBITDA was primarily due to higher organic sales volume of $57.1 million or 23.1%, and acquisitions of $18.6 million or 7.5%, partially offset by unfavorable cost inflation and product mix of $46.1 million or 18.6%, higher selling and administrative costs of $21.3 million or 8.6% and unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $12.7 million or 5.1%.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are increasing, driven by higher demand for certain products.

evidence.text: The increase in inventories was primarily due to additions to inventory due to increased demand for certain products.
evidence.subsection: Working Capital

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Capex is increasing, with investments in strategic plans and possible acquisitions.

evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures signal strategic investments and growth opportunities, which is a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with the company in compliance with debt covenants and manageable borrowing levels.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2023, we were in compliance with all of our debt covenants and no event of default had occurred or was ongoing.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable debt leverage with compliance suggests no immediate issues, but no significant improvement.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividend payments and possible share buybacks.

evidence.text: We may from time to time repurchase shares of our common stock in the open market at prevailing market prices (including through Rule 10b5-1 plans), in privately negotiated transactions, a combination thereof or through other transactions.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Maintained through dividends and possible buybacks, but no clear indication of expansion or contraction.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, driven by higher cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: Free cash flow increased $115.7 million to $147.9 million in the three month period ended March 31, 2023 from $32.2 million in the same three month period in 2022 due to increased cash provided by operating activities, partially offset by higher capital expenditures.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in free cash flow indicates strong cash generation.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is manageable, with no material changes to previously disclosed matters.

evidence.text: We believe that as of March 31, 2023, there have been no material changes to the environmental matters disclosed in our annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Environmental Matters

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No material changes, indicating stability but no positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating and contributing significantly to the overall increase in revenue and segment orders. While organic growth is strong, there are other factors such as acquisitions and foreign currency impacts that also play a role in the revenue growth.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues was primarily due to higher pricing, higher organic volumes, and acquisitions discussed above.

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Orders was due to organic growth of $129.0 million or 10.0% and acquisitions of $78.7 million or 6.1%, partially offset by the unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $50.2 million or 3.9%.

evidence.text: Revenues for the three month period ended March 31, 2023 were $1,629.3 million, an increase of $292.3 million, or 21.9%, compared to $1,337.0 million for the same three month period in 2022. factors.summary: Input costs are rising, which is creating margin pressure for the company. However, the company is managing these increases through pricing adjustments and productivity improvements, which have helped mitigate some of the cost inflation.

evidence.text: The increase in gross profit is primarily due to higher pricing, higher organic volumes, and acquisitions discussed above. The increase in gross profit as a percentage of revenues is primarily due to the benefits of pricing changes in excess of inflation in material and labor costs.

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Adjusted EBITDA was primarily due to higher pricing of $106.9 million or 43.2%, higher organic sales volume of $57.1 million or 23.1%, and acquisitions of $18.6 million or 7.5%, partially offset by unfavorable cost inflation and product mix of $46.1 million or 18.6%, higher selling and administrative costs of $21.3 million or 8.6% and unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $12.7 million or 5.1%.

factors.summary: The company faces significant foreign currency exposure, primarily due to a substantial portion of its revenues and costs being denominated in non-U.S. dollar currencies. This exposure has resulted in a negative impact on its results, with foreign exchange movements reducing Segment Revenues by $46.5 million or 4.5% compared to the previous year.

evidence.text: A significant portion of our revenues, approximately 55% for the three month period ended March 31, 2023, was denominated in currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Because much of our manufacturing facilities and labor force costs are outside of the United States, a significant portion of our costs are also denominated in currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in foreign exchange rates can therefore impact our results of operations and are quantified when significant to our discussion.
evidence.subsection: Foreign Currency Fluctuations

evidence.text: The unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $46.5 million or 4.5%. The percentage of Segment Revenues derived from aftermarket parts and service was 40.0% in the three month period ended March 31, 2023 compared to 40.9% in the same three month period in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022

factors.summary: The company has been actively engaging in mergers and acquisitions, which have contributed to revenue growth through accretive growth and expanding capabilities. These activities, while not individually significant, collectively contribute to the company's results and are relevant when comparing period-to-period performance.

evidence.text: Part of our strategy for growth is to acquire complementary businesses that provide access to new technologies or geographies or expand our offerings. While acquisitions, as discussed further in Note 3, are not individually significant or significant in the aggregate, they may be relevant when comparing our results from period to period.
evidence.subsection: Factors Affecting the Comparability of our Results of Operations / Acquisitions

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Revenues was due to higher pricing of $106.9 million or 10.3%, higher organic volumes of $147.3 million or 14.2%, and acquisitions of $69.9 million or 6.7%, partially offset by unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $46.5 million or 4.5%. The percentage of Segment Revenues derived from aftermarket parts and service was 40.0% in the three month period ended March 31, 2023 compared to 40.9% in the same three month period in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022

factors.summary: The impact of economic conditions on the company is mixed. While general economic conditions are acting as a headwind due to dependence on capital expenditures and maintenance spending, the company experienced strong demand and revenue growth during the three-month period ended March 31, 2023, driven by higher pricing, organic volumes, and acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our financial results closely follow changes in the industries and end-markets we serve. Demand for most of our products depends on the level of new capital investment and planned and unplanned maintenance expenditures by our customers. The level of capital expenditures depends, in turn, on the general economic conditions as well as access to capital at reasonable cost.
evidence.subsection: General Economic Conditions

evidence.text: The increase in Segment Revenues was due to higher pricing of $106.9 million or 10.3%, higher organic volumes of $147.3 million or 14.2%, and acquisitions of $69.9 million or 6.7%, partially offset by unfavorable impact of foreign currencies of $46.5 million or 4.5%. The percentage of Segment Revenues derived from aftermarket parts and service was 40.0% in the three month period ended March 31, 2023 compared to 40.9% in the same three month period in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Segment Results for the Three Month Periods Ended March 31, 2023 and 2022
